question.label: Settling check-in
question.name: What’s been most on your mind at work this week, Sam?
question.description: Gives Sam room to set the context before any friction is explored.

answer: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
note: He can do the tasks but is unsure whether his approach matches team expectations, which is a mild clarity gap.

question.label: Team expectations
question.name: When you second-guess your approach, what feels hardest to read about team expectations?
question.description: Drills into the specific expectation gap he just named so the manager can remove friction.

answer: Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
note: Mismatch between design and API details is still costing him time, which signals a mild ongoing clarity gap in how work boundaries line up.

question.label: Design API mismatch
question.name: When design and API details do not match, what is usually unclear first?
question.description: Drills into the specific mismatch he named so the manager can remove the most common source of confusion.

answer: I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
note: He names an approval-path gap before pushing, which is a mild negative clarity signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'who should approve what before I push' not drilled because drill cap requires advancing the arc.

question.label: Ticket expectations
question.name: What feels clear or unclear about what “done enough to ship” means here?
question.description: Checks whether his hesitation is coming from expectations rather than ability.

answer: I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
note: He describes delaying help-seeking because he is trying to solve things alone first, which strongly signals unclear shipping expectations and escalation norms.

question.label: Ask earlier
question.name: When you hold off asking, what tells you it is time to pull someone in?
question.description: Drills into his help-seeking threshold so the manager can make escalation expectations explicit.

answer: When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
note: He names specific, fast feedback as the condition that unlocks progress, which signals a strong clarity gap and some willingness to move once guidance is concrete.

question.label: Specific and fast
question.name: What kind of feedback helps most in those moments?
question.description: Drills into what 'specific and fast' means so support can be made concrete.

answer: I still feel fuzzy on where quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows.
note: He names a specific clarity gap around differing quality bars for quick fixes versus core flows, with mild drag on momentum from the uncertainty. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows' not drilled because drill cap reached and blockers stage is under-served.

question.label: Confidence trigger
question.name: What tends to make you pause before shipping a ticket?
question.description: Surfaces the concrete trigger behind late reassurance-seeking.

answer: Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent me from getting stuck silently.
note: Names missing pairing as the thing that would prevent getting stuck silently, which signals current friction and support gaps; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] pairing once or twice a week is a concrete support thread to pick up next session.

question.label: Support cadence
question.name: What kind of check-in from me would be most useful over the next two weeks?
question.description: Helps line up a lightweight support rhythm without over-managing.

answer: If we define what good looks like for my next two tickets, I will feel much steadier.
note: Names a specific missing support need — defining what good looks like on the next two tickets — which signals mild clarity and steadiness gaps during onboarding.

question.label: Two-week takeaway
question.name: What’s the one thing you want to have changed by our next check-in?
question.description: Closes on a concrete commitment for the next two weeks.

note: [SKIP] no signal — planner bypassed, queue carried forward
